rulings.Case Title: FOROUGH NADAF-RAHROV VS. NEIMAN MARCUS GROUP, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel The Continued Deposition Of Pltf Forough Nadaf-Rahrov
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, December 6, 2010, line 2, DEFENDANT NEIMAN MARCUS GROUP, INC. Motion To Compel The Continued Deposition Of Plaintiff Forough Nadaf-Rahrov. GRANTED. THE DEPOSITION IS REOPENED REGARDING (1)PLAINTIFF'S ABILITY TO PERFORM JOBS ON THE LIST PROVIDED BY DEFENDANT ON NOVEMBER 17, 2009; (2)PLAINTIFF'S ABILITY AND WILLINGNESS TO RELOCATE TO THE FACILITIES WHERE THOSE JOBS EXISTED; (3)PLAINTIFF'S MEDICAL CONDITION AND ABILITY TO WORK FROM AUGUST 6, 2005 THROUGH THE PRESENT; (AND) (4)HER EFFORTS TO MITIGATE DAMAGES SINCE HER DEPOSITION ON AUGUST 5, 2005. REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS DENIED, PLAINTIFF DEMONSTRATES SUBSTANTIAL JUSTIFICATION. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: GARY E GROTE et al VS. DAVID DRIVER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc And Motion Of Defts To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, December 6, 2010, line 3, DEFENDANTS DAVID DRIVER, KRISHNA RALLAPALLI, PHILIPPINE RALLAPALLI, RALLAPALLI, EMELIA Motion Of Defendants To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories. GRANTED IN PART, LATE OPPOSITION NOT CONSIDERED. WITHIN 5 DAYS OF NOTICE OF ENTRY OF THIS ORDER, PLAINTIFFS MUST PROVIDE FURTHER, COMPLETE AND VERIFIED RESPONSES TO FORM INTERROGATORIES AND PRODUCE ALL DOCUMENTS AND A PRIVILEGE LOG IN COMPLIANCE WITH THE COURT'S ORDER FILED 8-27-2010 AND SIGNED BY COMMISSIONER SLAVIT. PLAINTIFFS MUST IDENTIFY WHICH PLAINTIFFS OR THIRD PARTIES ARE PRODUCING WHICH DOCUMENTS AND PROVIDE A LETTER SUMMARY OF THE TOTALITY OF WHAT HAS BEEN DELIVERED. PLAINTIFFS ARE JOINTLY AND SEVERALLY ORDERED TO PAY DEFENDANTS MONETARY SANCTIONS IN THE AMOUNT OF $2,515.00. REQUESTS FOR EVIDENTIARY AND ISSUE SANCTIONS ARE DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: MONTGOMERY SANSOME LP VS. ZHIAN Z REZAI et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR ATTORNEY FEES AND EXPENSES
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, December 6, 2010, line 1, DEFENDANT ZHIAN Z. REZAI, INDIVIDUALLY AND AS TRUSTEE OF THE, JACKLYN J. REZAI (SUED HEREIN AS THE TRUSTEE MOTION FOR ATTORNEY FEES AND EXPENSES. GRANTED IN PART. DEFENDANT IS AWARDED REQUESTED FEES OF $127,616.90. STOKUS V. MARSH (1990) 217 CAL.APP.3D 647. rulings.Case Title: CELESTE BROWN VS. THE GYMBOREE CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Petition To Compel Arbitration And Stay Proceedings
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, December 6, 2010, line 4, DEFENDANTS THE GYMBOREE CORPORATION AND JOHN SHUNK Notice Of Petition To Compel Arbitration And Stay Proceedings. GRANTED, THIS CIVIL CASE IS STAYED PENDING COMPLETION OF ARBITRATION. THE PARTIES FORMED A VALID AND ENFORCEABLE AGREEMENT TO ARBITRATE THAT ENCOMPASSES THIS DISPUTE. THE "ANY AND ALL PAST OR PRESENT DISPUTES WITH ANYONE" LANGUAGE CAN BE FAIRLY READ TO INCLUDE ALL DISPUTES ARISING FROM THE EMPLOYMENT RELATIONSHIP WHEN THE ARBITRATION CLAUSE IS READ IN ITS ENTIRETY. THE AGREEMENT WAS ENTERED INTO AT THE BEGINNING OF PLAINTIFF'S EMPLOYMENT. ANY DOUBTS OR AMBIGUITIES AS TO THE SCOPE OF THE ARBITRATION CLAUSE ITSELF SHOULD BE RESOLVED IN FAVOR OF ARBITRATION. CRONUS INVESTMENTS, INC. V. CONCIERGE SERVICES (2005) 35 CAL.4TH 376, 386. THE AGREEMENT WAS NOT UNENFORCEABLE BECAUSE PLAINTIFF WAS REQUIRED TO SIGN IT AS A TERM OF HER EMPLOYMENT. LAGATREE V. LUCE, FORWARD, HAMILTON & SCRIPPS (1999) 74 CAL.APP.4TH 1105. THE FACT THAT THE CAA RULES ARE INCORPORATED BUT NOT ATTACHED MAY INDICATE SOME PROCEDURAL UNCONSCIONABILITY BUT DOES NOT MAKE THE AGREEMENT UNENFORCEABLE. THE CAA RULES ACTUALLY FAVOR THE EMPLOYEE AND PROVIDE PROCEDURAL SAFEGUARDS. SLIGHT PROCEDURAL UNCONSCIONABILITY WOULD REQUIRE A MORE OPPRESIVE SUBSTANTIVE UNCONSCIONABILITY TO RENDER THE ARBITRATION AGREEMENT UNENFORCEABLE. NO SUBSTANTIVE UNCONSCIONABILITY IS PRESENT HERE. THE AGREEMENT PROVIDES ALL OF THE SAFEGUARDS REQUIRED BY ARMENDARIZ, AND IS MUTUALLY BINDING ON BOTH PARTIES. BOTH PARTIES ARE PERMITTED TO APPLY FOR CIVIL INJUNCTIVE RELIEF AND DOES NOT EXEMPT CLAIMS FROM ARBITRATION THAT ARE ONLY LIKELY TO BE BROUGHT BY THE EMPLOYER, SUCH AS INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY VIOLATIONS. MCMANUS V. CIBC WORLD MARKETS, CORP. (2003) 109 CAL.APP.4TH 76. =(302/CWW)
